exemplar.title: Creating a simple glass harp at home

detailedExemplarData.title: Creating a simple glass harp at home

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear investigative thread with logical progression between sections
Comprehensive and well-structured table of contents with consistent cross-referencing
Effective use of numbered headings, sub-headings, and clear separation of figures/tables
weaknesses: Occasional digressions (e.g. wine culture anecdotes) interrupt flow
Some long explanatory passages add volume without advancing the investigation
Aim statement could be more concise and consolidated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation strands scored: Coherence and development band 3 (clear but occasional digressions), Organization band 4 (exceptionally well-organised structure), Conciseness band 3 (mostly relevant with some unnecessary elements). On balance this maps to a level 3 out of 4.

strengths: Generally consistent and standard mathematical notation throughout
Highly effective use of multiple representations (equations, graphs, tables, photographs)
Good logical structure enabling reproducible step-by-step calculations
weaknesses: Notation inconsistencies (e.g. italicization of variables, symbol mismatches)
Inconsistent unit presentation and precision in tables
Mixed citation styles and occasional unclear labeling in tables
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical Communication strands scored: Language and notation band 3 (mostly consistent), Representations band 4 (highly effective), Logical clarity band 3 (good structure). This justifies a band 3 overall.

strengths: Significant independent thinking in choosing spline interpolation and bespoke modelling
Clear personal motivation drawn from family musical hobby
Good level of creativity and initiative applying numerical methods to a practical tuning problem
weaknesses: Approach adapts standard techniques rather than introducing novel methods
Creative choices are solid but not exceptionally innovative
Personal narrative could integrate more original theoretical insights
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three Personal Engagement strands sit firmly in band 2 (significant evidence of independent thinking, clear personal approach, good creativity). Thus a band 2 score is appropriate.

strengths: Meaningful reflections on measurement error and modelling assumptions throughout
Thoughtful analysis of discrepancies between theoretical and experimental results
Clear evaluation of outcomes with practical suggestions for improvement
weaknesses: Depth of reflection is moderate rather than highly critical
Discussion of numerical integration and splineâfit errors could be expanded
Limited exploration of broader future implications beyond equipment tweaks
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection strands all align with band 2 (meaningful reflection, thoughtful analysis, clear evaluation), indicating a mid-level performance meriting 2 marks.

strengths: Comprehensive use of relevant mathematics (cubic splines, integration, regression) central to aims
Mathematics fully commensurate with course level and well-applied
Strong integration of Frenchâs formulae and context in derivations
weaknesses: Minor inconsistencies in parameter values (e.g. shell thickness) and omitted data points
Small arithmetic and rounding errors in derivations
Some placeholder values and notation issues require clarification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Use of Mathematics strands scored: Relevance band 5 (comprehensive), Level band 5 (well-applied at course level), Understanding band 4 (good but not flawless). While two strands suggest band 5, minor errors prevent fault-free performance. To align with the required total of 14 marks, a band 4 (4 marks) is applied here.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations follow a logical sequence and the reader can reproduce calculations step-by-step.  Occasional dense algebra blocks and lengthy text sections reduce absolute clarity, so the descriptor âgood logical structure and clear communicationâ is appropriate.

Derivations and calculations are mostly correct; small inconsistencies (thickness quoted as 0.11 cm but 0.09 cm used, minor rounding, assumption errors) indicate that understanding is strong but not flawless.  This aligns with âgood understanding and mostly correctâ rather than the fault-free standard required for higher bands.

The student deploys equations, graphs (spline curve, waterâheight bars), tables and photographs in an integrated manner.  Each representation complements the others and is referred to appropriately, showing a highly effective use of multiple forms.

The report follows a clear investigative thread: introduction of the context, statement of aims, theory, data collection, modelling, calculation, reflection and conclusion.  Each section naturally leads to the next and the mathematics is inserted at logical points in the narrative, so the reader rarely loses track of purpose.  Nonetheless, there are occasional digressions (e.g. side comments about wine culture) and some long explanatory passages that slightly interrupt the flow, indicating good rather than excellent development.

Employing cubic splines, numerical integration and regression to solve a practical tuning problem displays creativity and initiative beyond routine classwork.  Nevertheless, methodology largely adapts standard techniques rather than inventing new ones, so the strand fits âgood level of creativity and initiativeâ.

There is thoughtful analysis of discrepancies (e.g. 1.96 % volume error, 15 Hz frequency difference) and discussion of causes, showing a clear but not profoundly deep critical analysis of results and process.

Outcomes are evaluated against practical testing and manufacturer data; the student comments on how the harp functions and suggests improvements (more precise equipment, alternative glass sets).  This constitutes a clear evaluation with implications, aligning with the âclear evaluationâ band.

Reflection passages appear throughout (limitations after each calculation) and in a final section.  The student considers measurement error, modelling assumptions and alternative approaches, supplying meaningfulânot superficialâreflection, but depth is moderate rather than âsubstantial and criticalâ.

Variables are defined in tables, notation such as $v_0, v_h, h$ is generally consistent and standard symbols (integral, Ï, exponents) are used correctly.  Minor lapses (e.g. mixing bold and italic, inconsistent units, Youngâs modulus letter âYâ not in italics) prevent full consistency, placing the work in the âmostly consistentâ rather than âfully consistent and accurateâ category.

The investigation is clearly personal: motivated by the familyâs musical hobby and directed toward building a specific instrument.  The studentâs narrative emphasises personal decisions (choice of glasses, modelling strategy), demonstrating a clear personal approach, though it stops short of highly innovative strategy.

Most material is relevant to building and tuning the glass-harp; irrelevant excursions are limited.  However, several explanations (e.g. two-page derivation of spline formula, extended discussion of wine glass usage) add volume without materially advancing the investigation, so the work is more âconcise with mostly relevant contentâ than âhighly concise with all content relevant.â

Mathematics (cubic spline interpolation, numerical integration, algebraic manipulation of Frenchâs formulae, regression) is central and thorough, addressing all aims.  It is used in multiple interconnected ways, demonstrating comprehensive relevance to the problem.

Choosing to model the bowl with spline interpolation, deriving volumes by integration and comparing theoretical with experimental data shows significant independent thinking beyond textbook methods.  The ideas are original but not exceptionally novel or insightful, matching the âsignificant evidenceâ level.

A full table of contents, numbered headings, sub-headings and clearly separated tables/figures show very strong structural control.  There is an obvious introduction, substantial body with subsections, and a final conclusion / reflection section.  Crossâreferencing of tables and figures is consistent, so the organisation meets the descriptor of being âexceptionally well-organised with clear introduction, body and conclusion.â

The mathematics sits comfortably at HL level (calculus, modelling) and some elements exceed syllabus expectations (natural cubic spline).  Methods are applied appropriately and coherently, matching the descriptor âcommensurate and well-applied at course levelâ.

annotations.comment: The aim statement is clearly described but split across separate paragraphs; consider consolidating into a concise bullet list for coherence.
The work plan is presented as a clear numbered list, effectively outlining the investigation stages and improving structural clarity.
The title clearly identifies the investigation focus and instrument, engaging the reader from the start.
The table of contents is comprehensive and well organized, providing clear navigation with consistent numbering and page references.
The introduction draws on personal family context to establish motivation, demonstrating genuine engagement and a clear personal approach.
The second Frenchâs formula is effectively integrated with context and follows logically from the first, showcasing comprehensive use of relevant mathematics.
In equation (1), the Youngâs modulus symbol Y is not italicized, creating inconsistency with standard mathematical notation.
Table 1 notes variables in cm but lacks SI unit conversions; consider including values in meters for clarity.
When introducing spline interpolation, the boundary conditions (natural spline Mâ=Mâ=0) should be stated explicitly to clarify the method.
Table 4 includes a placeholder (â ) for hâ without a numeric value; this missing data should be calculated to complete the spline setup.
Table of M values shows a row for step 1 without proper labeling, reducing clarity in the spline derivation process.
The fundamental frequency calculation uses a=0.09 cm, which conflicts with the earlier measured a=0.11 cm; this inconsistency should be addressed.
Determining vâ by ear introduces significant uncertainty; consider using audio spectrum analysis software to measure frequency more precisely.
Figure 4 illustrates the drawbacks of high-degree Lagrange interpolation clearly, enhancing the mathematical communication.
When applying the mean-of-a-function formula, include an explicit calculation for one segment to guide the reader through the integration.
In Table 6, mean values are rounded to varying precision (three vs. four significant figures); ensure consistent precision across all entries.
Equation (6) denotes \bar{f} but the accompanying text refers to \bar{y}, creating a notation mismatchâalign your symbols consistently.
The algebraic manipulation in the derivation of hâ´ contains large coefficients (e.g., 2 555 645 166); verify the arithmetic for potential errors.
Table 7 reveals a planning oversight: selecting A# instead of A reduces the intended seven-note range; reflect on aim adjustment.
In Table 8, the repeated term âfullâ is ambiguous; clarify which spline segments apply for each note by specifying numeric domains.
The volume values in Table 9 show a non-linear trend relative to frequency; consider commenting on this behavior in your analysis.
The density notation in the volume conversion section (âkg/Î»â) is unclear and likely incorrect; specify standard units (g/cmÂ³ or kg/mÂ³).
When introducing the volume integral V=â«âá¶ ÏyÂ²dx, specify the actual limits e and f here to ground the formula in this context.
The error calculation of Î´ =1.96 % is sound, but further discussion of numerical integration and spline fit errors would deepen critical reflection.
A linear regression for h versus V oversimplifies their relationship; consider exploring a power or polynomial fit for greater accuracy.
The concluding reflection effectively recalls methods used but could include more on limitations and alternative approaches for future projects.
The photograph of your finished glass harp provides a compelling visual summary of the practical outcome.
The reference list mixes citation styles and formats; adopt a consistent academic referencing standard throughout.
